behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watching semantics (recursive/nested path events), which are OS/watcher dependent. MOCK-MISS is cross-module (watcher event -> path resolution -> pipeline enqueue), but REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE push this to System because mocked watcher events can hide real recursive-watch gaps. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text is purely filesystem processing with no AI/ML decision steps.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp input/output directory tree. Create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file into it, and assert one transformed output is produced for that nested file path without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets dedup rule correctness for same path + time window, which can fail inside one local dedup component (keying/time-window comparison) without requiring OS or network. REJECTION-GATE for Integration/System: no behavior text requiring real filesystem event emission; it says 'processing request', which can be invoked directly in-process. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup component/function directly with a controllable clock: first request for path is accepted, second request for same path within window is rejected, and a request after window is accepted again.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS multi-event emission; exact failure appears at real OS watcher boundary. Mocked events can miss coalescing/duplicate patterns seen in real watchers, so MOCK-HIDE applies. Rejected lower levels by concrete text: 'operating system emits multiple filesystem events.' Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watched directory, perform one write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., write+rename/atomic save), capture processing records, and assert exactly one processing/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is a live OS filesystem lifecycle condition (delete/recreate directory) plus long-running watcher recovery. In-process fakes often miss real watch-handle invalidation/rebind behavior, so System is required. Rejected lower levels by concrete text: 'directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running.' Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML path.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory. Remove the watched directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real arrival concurrency plus filesystem creation-time metadata and event timing. A pure in-process queue test can validate sorting logic but can hide real failures in timestamp acquisition/event race at OS boundary; thus REAL-INFRA elevates to System. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no LLM stages.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real input directory, create multiple files nearly simultaneously with controlled creation times, then assert processing sequence matches filesystem creation timestamps rather than raw event arrival order.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is tied to real partial-write timing and filesystem event behavior while a file handle is still active. Mocked writes/events can trivially pass and hide truncation/read-before-complete bugs, so MOCK-HIDE and REAL-INFRA require System. Rejected lower levels by concrete text: 'still being written to when the arrival event fires.' Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real directory, start writing a large file in chunks with delays, emit/trigger early arrival event naturally via watcher, and assert processing starts only after final chunk/close, producing complete output.
